mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is falling or already low, equity discount rates decline and risk appetite improves, lifting S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal is valid only if inflation is not accelerating at the same time, because renewed inflation pressure can force a repricing of rate expectations.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive on average when FEDFUNDS is flat-to-down and CPIAUCSL inflation is decelerating, or if the signal loses directional accuracy below 55% in a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden rise in UNRATE marks labor-market deterioration that weakens household income expectations and corporate revenue outlooks, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following month. The effect is strongest when payroll growth is also slowing, because it confirms broad labor softening rather than a one-off unemployment uptick.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes do not produce negative average 20-day SPX returns, or if positive returns occur in more than 60% of instances despite a confirmed unemployment surge and payroll slowdown.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO signals stronger industrial supply-side activity and better cyclical earnings momentum, which supports S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The mechanism fails when industrial production rises only because of inventory rebuilding without accompanying broad demand strength.
failure_condition: Reject if positive month-over-month INDPRO growth does not coincide with above-baseline 60-day SPX returns, or if the rolling 3-year correlation between INDPRO changes and forward SPX returns stays at or below zero.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rebound in RSAFS indicates improving nominal household and business spending, which lifts revenue expectations and supports S&P 500 gains over the next month. The signal breaks when sales growth is purely inflation-driven and CPIAUCSL is accelerating, because real demand then is not improving.
failure_condition: Reject if two-month RSAFS increases are not followed by positive average 20-day SPX returns, or if the signal fails when CPIAUCSL inflation is stable-to-falling and returns remain nonpositive.

mechanism_narrative: A low and falling UNRATE regime supports equities because stable employment reduces recession odds and sustains consumption, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next week. This short-horizon effect should weaken once unemployment is already near cyclical lows and cannot improve further.
failure_condition: Reject if a declining UNRATE does not produce higher-than-baseline 5-day SPX returns, or if the sign flips negative during periods when UNRATE is falling and recession risk is otherwise absent.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS exceeds a restrictive threshold, valuation pressure and financial conditions tighten, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The effect is strongest if CPIAUCSL remains elevated, because the market then expects policy to stay restrictive longer.
failure_condition: Reject if periods with FEDFUNDS above 4.5% do not show negative average 60-day SPX returns, or if elevated policy rates with high CPIAUCSL still coincide with sustained equity gains in most triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in HOUST signals weaker interest-sensitive demand and softer future consumption spillovers, which should reduce S&P 500 returns over the next month. The mechanism is most informative when housing starts fall alongside softer retail spending, confirming that demand is broad-based rather than sector-specific.
failure_condition: Reject if sharp HOUST declines are not followed by negative 20-day SPX returns, or if triggered cases show positive returns more than 60% of the time despite concurrent RSAFS weakness.

mechanism_narrative: A persistent rise in INDPRO after a prior dip reflects a manufacturing reacceleration that improves earnings revisions and risk sentiment, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The signal fails if industrial production rises only while PAYEMS stagnates, because output gains without hiring do not confirm a durable recovery.
failure_condition: Reject if simultaneous improvement in INDPRO and PAYEMS does not precede positive 60-day SPX returns, or if the signal loses predictive power in periods where industrial gains are not matched by employment gains.
